holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is +9.8% over the past week and sitting at $132.94 — a name that's clearly caught a momentum bid, but there's no dated company catalyst on the board to anchor an add/trim window. With nothing but triple witching ~84 days out as macro noise, the honest read is to let the tape define the next setup: either confirmation of the breakout with follow-through, or a fade that gives the next entry. No earnings or ex-div date means no edge in pre-positioning today.
INTC.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no INTC-specific dated catalyst provided)
INTC.bull: 5-day momentum (+9.8%) suggests the turnaround narrative is regaining sponsorship; trend-followers carry it further absent a negative print.
INTC.bear: A 10% rip in a week with no dated catalyst to defend it is the classic setup for a give-back into any tape weakness or sector rotation.

NVDA.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for NVDA right now — next earnings isn't even on the calendar yet, and the only thing ahead is triple witching 84 days out, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. The 8-share position is down 1.5% on the day and 4.3% on the week, so it's pulling back but not in capitulation territory; without a catalyst to define the window, there's no edge in adding or trimming here. Wait for the next earnings date to get posted (or a dated GTC/product event) before leaning either way.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not NVDA-specific)
NVDA.bull: AI capex cycle still intact and a ~4% pullback in a leadership name often resolves higher absent a dated negative catalyst.
NVDA.bear: No near-term print to defend the stock if tape weakens, and triple-witching positioning can amplify drawdowns in crowded megacaps.

ORCL.read: ORCL is in a sharp drawdown — down 16.8% over five sessions and another 3.1% today — with no company-dated catalyst on the board to anchor a window. Without a confirmed earnings date or other binary event, this is a tape read, not an event read: the question is whether selling exhausts before the next print resets the narrative. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a flow event, not an ORCL-specific catalyst.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event, not ORCL-specific)
ORCL.bull: A 17% drawdown in a megacap AI-infra name often sets up a mean-revert bounce once forced selling clears.
ORCL.bear: No dated company catalyst to stop the bleed; momentum breaks like this tend to overshoot before stabilizing.

NOK.read: NOK is grinding higher quietly (+1% on the day and week) with no dated company catalyst on the board — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. With nothing binary to lean into, this is a watch: the next real window opens when Nokia confirms Q3 earnings (typically late October), and until then the tape is just slow drift on a 174-share position that isn't large enough to actively manage around triple witching.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific dated catalyst on the board)
NOK.bull: Quiet uptrend with no event risk near-term; 5G/AI networking narrative can carry the tape into the next earnings confirmation.
NOK.bear: Without a dated catalyst, the position is exposed to broad-tape risk into September op-ex with no company-specific upside trigger.

NBIS.read: Tiny 1-share NBIS position with no company-specific dated catalysts in the feed — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div, no guidance event to anchor a window. The only thing on the calendar is triple witching on 2026-09-18, which is a flows event, not an NBIS event, so there's nothing here that defines a real add/trim moment. Until an earnings date or AI-capex/customer update gets confirmed, this is a watch, not an action.
NBIS.bull: Neocloud/AI-infra demand narrative remains intact and any confirmed capacity or hyperscaler deal headline reprices the name fast.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI-infra name with no near-term dated catalyst means it trades on sentiment and can get tagged hard on any AI-spend wobble or index-flow day.

KO.read: Tiny 5-share KO position sitting basically flat (-0.21% on the day, +0.64% on the week) with no company-specific dated catalyst on the calendar — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the feed. The only thing ahead is triple witching in mid-September, which is a tape event, not a KO event, so there's no real edge to act on here. This is a coupon-clipping staple with no window open right now.
KO.bull: Defensive staple with stable dividend; price action is calm and constructive, no distribution signal.
KO.bear: No dated company catalyst means no asymmetric setup — capital is parked, not working, and any tape-wide vol around Sept witching could drag it.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a freshly public name (Jun 2026 IPO) with no confirmed earnings or dividend dates in the feed yet — coverage hasn't caught up, so there's no dated catalyst to trade around. Position is tiny (10 shares) and the only thing on the calendar is Sep 18 triple witching, which is a flow event, not a fundamental one. Right move is to wait until the first post-IPO print gets calendared before forming an add/trim window.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (flow event; no SPCX-specific catalyst dated yet)
SPCX.bull: First-as-public-co print would mark SpaceX's debut earnings narrative and could re-rate the name on Starlink/Starship disclosures.
SPCX.bear: Newly public, lock-up dynamics and uncovered estimates mean any unscheduled disclosure or supply unlock can gap the stock with no warning.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
